1.texto_original: Y Jehová habló a Moisés en los campos de Moab, junto al Jordán [frente a] Jericó, diciendo:
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Números 35:1

"Y Jehová habló a Moisés en los campos de Moab, junto al Jordán [frente a] Jericó, diciendo:"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Números 35:1

Philological Analysis

"Y Jehová habló a Moisés en los campos de Moab, junto al Jordán [frente a] Jericó, diciendo:"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Y Jehová habló a Moisés en los campos de Moab, junto al Jordán [frente a] Jericó, diciendo:"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Números 35:15

Philological Analysis

"Estas seis ciudades serán para refugio a los hijos de Israel, y al peregrino, y al que morare entre ellos, para que huya allá cualquiera que hiriere de muerte a otro por yerro."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Y estas cosas os serán por ordenanza de derecho por vuestras edades, en todas vuestras habitaciones."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

30.texto_original: Cualquiera que hiriere a alguno, por dicho de testigos, morirá el homicida: mas un solo testigo no hará fe contra alguna persona para que muera.
30.normal:
Devotional Study of Números 35:30

"Cualquiera que hiriere a alguno, por dicho de testigos, morirá el homicida: mas un solo testigo no hará fe contra alguna persona para que muera."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
30.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Números 35:30

Philological Analysis

"Cualquiera que hiriere a alguno, por dicho de testigos, morirá el homicida: mas un solo testigo no hará fe contra alguna persona para que muera."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

32.texto_original: Ni tampoco tomaréis precio del que huyó a su ciudad de refugio, para que vuelva a vivir en [su] tierra, hasta que muera el sacerdote.

33.texto_original: Y no contaminaréis la tierra donde estuviereis: porque esta sangre amancillará la tierra; y la tierra no será expiada de la sangre que fue derramada en ella, sino por la sangre del que la derramó.
33.normal:
Devotional Study of Números 35:33

"Y no contaminaréis la tierra donde estuviereis: porque esta sangre amancillará la tierra; y la tierra no será expiada de la sangre que fue derramada en ella, sino por la sangre del que la derramó."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

34.texto_original: No contaminéis, pues, la tierra donde habitáis, en medio de la cual yo habito; porque yo Jehová habito en medio de los hijos de Israel.

Devotional Study of Números 35:34

"No contaminéis, pues, la tierra donde habitáis, en medio de la cual yo habito; porque yo Jehová habito en medio de los hijos de Israel."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.
